# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 97.046 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.8265
  biquad b0=1.00060263684 b1=-1.99703810607 b2=0.996475753476 a1=-1.99703810607 a2=0.997078390318
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 71.045 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 2.4475
  biquad b0=1.00032946283 b1=-1.99838114579 b2=0.998073287017 a1=-1.99838114579 a2=0.998402749845
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 46.875 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.5607
  biquad b0=1.00034088921 b1=-1.99833794963 b2=0.998006465021 a1=-1.99833794963 a2=0.998347354234
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 15.015 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.3063
  biquad b0=1.00013052207 b1=-1.99936625791 b2=0.999236701244 a1=-1.99936625791 a2=0.999367223314

  biquad b0=1.00052007772 b1=-1.99747207154 b2=0.996958562839 a1=-1.99747207154 a2=0.997478640556
Filter 6: ON PK Fc 19.409 Hz Gain +2.103 dB Q 1.7706
  biquad b0=1.00008703988 b1=-1.99936292615 b2=0.999277499496 a1=-1.99936292615 a2=0.999364539376
